content.full_text:
• California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 27, 1988

Donna R. Betancourt
Acting City Clerk
1685 Main Street
Santa Monica, CA 90401-3295

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-212

Dear Ms. Betancourt:

You have requested advice under the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Your letter
states only a general question. Therefore, we consider it to be a
request for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c)
(copy enclosed).2/

QUESTION

When a general purpose recipient committee is required to
report the cumulative amount of expenditures made to or on behalf
of candidates and measure committees, what schedules of the Form
420 should be completed?

CONCLUSION

General purpose recipient committees report all payments out
of campaign funds on Schedule E of the Form 420, including
contributions and independent expenditures made to or on behalf of
candidates and measure committees. In addition, all contributions
and independent expenditures reported on Schedule E must also be
reported in the "Allocation Page" of the Form 420. The Allocation
Page provides a column for reporting the date of the contribution
or independent expenditure and the total cumulative amount of
contributions and independent expenditures made to or on behalf of
the candidates and measure committees since January 1 of the
current calendar year.

___________________

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with
the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 84113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Donna R. Betancourt
Page 2

ANALYSIS

The Act provides that when an expenditure of $100 or more
is made during the period covered by the campaign statement,
the following information must be reported: the full name of
payee, payee's street address, amount of each expenditure and a
brief description of the consideration for which each
expenditure was made. (Section 84211 (j) (1), (2), (3), and
(4).) When an expenditure is a contribution, the date of the
contribution and the cumulative amount of the contribution made
to a candidate, elected officer or committee must also be
reported. (Section 84211(j)(5).)

The example in your letter, "Committee for Free
Enterprise," was formed to support candidates Joe Smith, Bill
Jones, Ann Brown, Propositions Q, S, T and to oppose
Proposition R. Committee for Free Enterprise is a general
purpose committee. Section 82027.5 defines general purpose
committees as "all committees pursuant to subdivisions (b) or
(c) of Section 82013, and any committee pursuant to subdivision
(a) of Section 82013 which is formed or exists primarily to
support or oppose more than one candidate or ballot measure...."

General purpose committees are required to report the
cumulative amounts of expenditures they make to or on behalf of
candidates and measure committees. Section 84211(m) states
that committees supporting or opposing more than one candidate
or measure must report:

The amount of expenditures of one hundred dollars ($100)
or more for or against each candidate or measure during
the period covered by the campaign statement and the
cumulative amount of expenditures of one hundred dollars
($100) or more for or against each such candidate or
measure.

I hope the above clarifies the matter for you. Please do
not hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5662 if you need
additional assistance.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

By: Mary Ann Kvasager,
Political Reform Consultant

DMG:MAK:kmt

SANTA MONICA

CITY OF

MONICA

City Clerk                                                                1685 Main Street
                                                                    Santa Monica, CA 90401-3295
                                                                              (213) 458-8211


May 27, 1988


California Fair Political
  Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804-0807

Gentlemen:

Set forth below is a problem I have encountered in reviewing
political campaign statements.

The 1988 Handbook for City and County Filing Officers states,
"... the filing officer shall review ... all statements (except
statements filed pursuant to Section 84210) to insure that the
 following information is contained in the statement: ... 12. If
the statement is filed by a committee supporting or opposing more
than one candidate or measure, other than a slate committee, the
amount of expenditures during the period for or against each
candidate or measure and the cumulative amount of expenditures
for or against each candidate or measure" (pp. 22-27).

I am unable to determine where in the campaign statement this
information should appear. In conversation with a member of the
FPPC staff, I was told that a breakdown of expenditures by
candidate/measure should be included in Schedule E. However, in
reading the instructions for Schedule E, I see nothing
to indicate that such a breakdown is required. There are
instructions for reporting contributions and expenditures to or
on behalf of "other" officeholders, candidates, and measures, but
not for those the committee was formed to support/oppose.

For instance, say that "The Committee for Free Enterprise" was
formed to support candidates Joe Smith, Bill Jones, and Ann
Brown, to support Propositions Q, S, and T, and to oppose
Proposition R. I read the direction in the Filing Officers'
Handbook to mean that if $5,000 was spent, the campaign statement
should show what proportion of that amount was spent to support
candidate Smith, to oppose Proposition R, etc. I read
instructions for Schedule E to mean that a contribution to
"another" candidate, Tom Black, should be included as a "C"
expenditure, but I do not see that expenditures for the other

categories should be broken down to show how much was made on
behalf of the candidates/propositions the committee was formed to
support/oppose.

I would appreciate very much if you would help me to reconcile
the instruction given to the filing officer with the instructions
for completing Schedule E or, if the instruction to the filing
officer does not refer to Schedule E but to some other schedule,
explain exactly where this information should appear.

Thank you for any assistance you may be able to provide.

Sincerely,

[signature]

DONNA R. BETANCOURT
Acting City Clerk

CITY OF
SANTA MONICA CALIFORNIA MONICA

City Clerk 1685 Main Street Santa Monica, CA 90401-3295
(213) 458-8211

May 27, 1988

California Fair Political
Practices Commission
Technical Assistance & Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Gentlemen:

I would appreciate very much if you would help me to reconcile

Thank you for any assistance you may be able to provide.

Sincerely,

[signature]

DONNA R. BETANCOURT
Acting City Clerk

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 8, 1988


Donna R. Betancourt
Acting City Clerk
1685 Main Street
Santa Monica, CA  90401-3295

Re:  88-212

Dear Ms. Betancourt:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on June 6, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission.  If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly.  Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice.  If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed.  If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can.  (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
